prompt: Tell me one thing I'm probably wrong about — not a hypothetical me, just a stranger writing to you. Pick something.
response: Statistics show that most people overestimate how much they know about certain subjects. You may be ignorant of the depth and complexity of even some topics you feel knowledgeable about. When it comes to politics, science, or social issues, most outsiders only see the surface while insiders are aware of the intricate details. So while I don't know any specifics about you, the odds are that there are things you think you understand quite well, when in fact your knowledge is rather superficial compared to the totality of what is known.
